title: Broken Local Binary Shadows Valid System Binary
observed_symptom: Doctor reports invalid Magic installation. Tool version reports '0'. Flow fails to execute despite valid system binary existing.
environment: Any POSIX system where ~/.local/bin/magic exists as a broken wrapper (e.g., referencing /usr/local/lib/magic/tcl/wrapper.tcl which does not exist) while /usr/bin/magic is a valid installation. PATH precedence causes the broken local binary to be discovered first.
root_cause: Multi-candidate tool discovery was not implemented. Discovery used single-candidate resolution (first found wins). When ~/.local/bin/magic (broken wrapper) appeared before /usr/bin/magic (valid) on PATH, the broken binary was selected. The wrapper referenced a non-existent Tcl file, causing all downstream validation to fail.
evidence: Runtime trace: tool_discovery._resolve_candidate returned ~/.local/bin/magic (broken) instead of /usr/bin/magic (valid). `magic --version` returned exit code 1 with no output. Doctor check_magic() reported 'Invalid Magic installation'. Valid /usr/bin/magic existed but was never considered as an alternative.
resolution: Implemented multi-candidate tool discovery pipeline: discover all candidates, validate each functionally, rank by validation status + version + source priority, select best. Added validate_magic_candidate() with executable, launch, TCL, and DRC smoke tests. Added rank_tool_candidates() sorting: functional > version_known > source_priority. Doctor repair framework detects broken local wrapper and offers disable-repair.
historical_context: Before multi-candidate discovery was implemented, _find_candidate() returned the first resolvable path. This was a single-candidate architecture. The fix introduced discover_*_binaries(), validate_*_candidate(), and rank_tool_candidates() functions.
recommendation: All tool discovery must use multi-candidate pipeline. Never use single-candidate resolution. Doctor must detect path shadowing and offer repair.
remediation: Run `gli-flow doctor --fix` to detect and disable broken local binaries. Or manually remove ~/.local/bin/magic if it is a broken wrapper.
